concerns.text: Tasks T036, T037, and T038 are explicitly marked [DEFERRED] with notes stating they are 'statistically invalid' due to dataset count. These tasks directly implement Success Criteria SC-001, SC-002, and SC-003 (Median/IQR reporting). Deferring the implementation of mandatory Success Criteria constitutes a silent weakening of the spec's deliverables, violating FR-012 and the requirement that tasks must not relax what the spec demands.

Task T009 acknowledges a 'SPEC DEVIATION' by using UCI datasets instead of the OpenML collection mandated by FR-001. While the plan notes this gap, the task itself implements a deliverable that falls short of the FR it claims to satisfy (FR-001). This is a semantic weakening of the functional requirement without an explicit spec amendment or kickback resolution reflected in the task's acceptance criteria.

Task T027 notes a correction to the spec (FDR vs FWER) within the task description but does not update the implementation to match the corrected logic or flag the spec requirement FR-007 as unmet. Implementing Benjamini-Hochberg (FDR) to satisfy a requirement explicitly asking for 'family-wise error rate' (FWER) control is a semantic weakening of the constraint, as the statistical guarantee differs.

Tasks T028 and T029 implement sensitivity analysis binning (FR-008) but include notes admitting that with only 2 datasets, the requirement for '≥1 dataset per bin' is impossible to meet. The tasks proceed to implement the logic but effectively deliver a partial result that fails the acceptance criterion of FR-008, constituting a silent relaxation of the constraint.
